greet.text: She studies you across the garage's cordite quiet — your face, her face, the mint under the coin. Up close she's smaller than the screens and steadier than the magazines, and her eyes do a thing you recognize from mirrors: inventorying the damage, saving the feelings for later. "They made you into me," she says. Not a question. "Does it hurt?"

options.text: "The surgery? No. The rest of it, kind of."
"You're asking if I'M okay? Lady, they were going to—"

protect.text: "I know what they were going to do." She says it evenly, and the evenness costs her something you can see being paid. "I've been dreaming this garage for a week — you were in the dreams, and I couldn't ever warn you, and I hated that part the most." She glances at the mirror-eyed woman, then back. "Nobody spends you tonight. That's done. Come north with us — I think... I think you're supposed to come north. The dreams have you in them there, too."

honest.text: A short, real laugh — HER laugh, the famous one, and it's so much better unrehearsed. "Honest. Good. Everyone I know is paid to lie to me; I collect honest people now." She looks at your shared face once more and something settles in her, a decision with roots. "I can't give you your old face back. But nobody spends you tonight, and where we're going, being me is my job again. You'll just be — new. Is Mona right? Come north, Mona."

bye.text: She slides over to make room like it's nothing, like she isn't the most protected face on Earth sitting in an unscheduled car with her own ghost — and pats the seat, once, impatient. The century's strangest sisterhood pulls out of the garage doing ninety.
